title: Alchemilla vulgaris genuina
taxonomy: Alchemilla vulgaris genuina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the rose family. This plant is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. Alchemilla vulgaris genuina is also known as lady's mantle and is often found growing in moist, woodland areas. It is a member of the genus Alchemilla, which contains around 200 species of plants. Alchemilla vulgaris genuina is widely cultivated for its attractive, chartreuse-colored flowers and its ability to thrive in a variety of environments.
uses: Alchemilla vulgaris genuina, also known as lady's mantle, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's leaves and flowers are sometimes used to treat digestive issues, such as stomach ulcers and diarrhea. Additionally, lady's mantle may have antioxidant properties, which could help protect against cell damage and promote overall health. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential effects.

side_effects: Alchemilla vulgaris genuina, also known as lady's mantle, has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. However, its use may lead to side effects such as digestive issues and allergic reactions in some individuals. The plant may also interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. Additionally, excessive consumption of Alchemilla vulgaris genuina may cause kidney damage in rare cases.
